metro_lines.name: Blue Line (North-South)
metro_lines.data_source: Kolkata Metro Railway Corporation (KMRC) / Indian Railways Metro Railway Kolkata ridership data 2023-24; India's oldest metro system (1984)

metro_lines.name: Green Line (East-West)
metro_lines.data_source: KMRC ridership data 2023-24; partial opening, full line under construction including underwater tunnel under Hooghly river

lines.name: Sealdah Division
lines.corridor: Multiple corridors from Sealdah: Sealdah-Ranaghat, Sealdah-Budge Budge, Sealdah-Diamond Harbour, Sealdah-Lakshmikantapur
lines.data_source: Eastern Railway Sealdah Division ridership data 2023-24

lines.name: Howrah Division
lines.corridor: Multiple corridors from Howrah: Howrah-Burdwan, Howrah-Kharagpur, Howrah-Amta
lines.data_source: South Eastern Railway and Eastern Railway Howrah Division ridership data 2023-24

suburban_rail.data_source: Combined Sealdah + Howrah suburban division ridership; Kolkata suburban rail is the second largest after Mumbai

tram.note: Kolkata is the only city in India with an operational tram network (since 1902, CTC operated). Heritage service, minimal transport role but culturally significant.
tram.data_source: Calcutta Tramways Company (CTC) operational data 2023-24; severely reduced from peak of 60+ routes

bus_network.operator: WBTC (West Bengal Transport Corporation) + Private operators
bus_network.data_source: WBTC and private bus operator aggregate data 2023-24; Kolkata bus network is a mix of state-run (WBTC/NBSTC) and extensive private bus operators

transport_network.total_daily_pt_ridership_data_source: Sum of metro (0.675M) + suburban rail (2.85M) + tram (0.008M) + bus (3.2M); Kolkata has second-highest suburban rail dependency after Mumbai

economic_baseline.data_source: Median income estimated from NSSO PLFS 2022-23 Kolkata urban MPCE scaled to household level (lowest among 6 cities); Gini from consumption expenditure distribution (lowest inequality, reflecting more compressed income distribution); formal employment from EPFO enrollment data; sectors from West Bengal Economic Review 2023-24

validation_anchors_note: To be populated post-Kolkata calibration; potential anchors include Metro Blue Line Dakshineswar extension ridership and WBTC route rationalization impact
